document.title: Professional Extension Catalog — Heritage Gold → LAWIM_V2
document.description: Enriched catalog of all professional extension models for LAWIM_V2 — covering professional profiles, service capabilities, licensing, certification, geographic coverage, availability, ratings, verification, pricing models, SLAs, and organization membership hierarchy across 27 professional categories

professional_categories.description: 27 professional categories organized by domain with hierarchy levels (Domain → Category → Specialty → Skill)

values.description: Licensed property transaction intermediary — buy, sell, rent, lease
Legal officer for property deeds, contracts, and authentications
Land surveying, boundary delineation, topographic studies
Building design, construction oversight, permit facilitation
Structural engineering, construction calculations, project management
Electrical installation, repair, maintenance, and wiring
Plumbing systems, water supply, drainage, and sanitation
Masonry, brickwork, concrete structures, and foundation work
Woodwork, framing, cabinetry, doors, windows, and joinery
Interior and exterior painting, surface preparation, finishes
Tile installation for floors, walls, and decorative surfaces
Roofing installation, repair, waterproofing, and gutter systems
Property valuation, market analysis, investment feasibility studies
Formal property appraisal for mortgage, insurance, and transaction purposes
Condominium association management, common area maintenance, fee collection
Aerial photography, videography, 3D mapping, property inspection from above
Mortgage brokerage, insurance brokerage, financial product intermediation
Property security, surveillance, access control, and alarm monitoring
Document processing, permit facilitation, administrative form submission
Legal representation, contract review, property litigation, advice
Accounting, bookkeeping, tax filing, financial statements
Insurance products including property, auto, health, and life insurance
Banking services, mortgage origination, financing advisory
Real estate development, construction project management, land development
Interior design, space planning, color consulting, furniture selection
Landscaping, garden maintenance, tree trimming, outdoor design
Swimming pool construction, maintenance, and renovation

pricing_models.description: 10 pricing models supporting diverse professional service monetization strategies

values.label: Fixed Price
values.description: Single fixed price for a defined service deliverable

values.label: Per Unit
values.description: Price per measurable unit (m², m³, room, hour, etc.)

values.label: Hourly
values.description: Hourly rate for time-based services

values.label: Daily
values.description: Daily rate, typically for construction teams

values.label: Percentage
values.description: Percentage of transaction or project value

values.label: Estimate
values.description: Custom quote based on site visit and scope evaluation

values.label: Subscription
values.description: Recurring monthly or annual fee for ongoing service

values.label: Tiered
values.description: Multiple service tiers (Basic/Standard/Premium) at different price points

values.label: Negotiable
values.description: Open to negotiation, typically for large or complex projects

values.label: Retainer
values.description: Monthly retainer for ongoing advisory or legal services

verification_levels.description: 4-level verification system ensuring professional authenticity and capability

levels.label: Document
privileges: Full search visibility with verification badge

levels.label: Premium
privileges: Top placement in search and matching

sla_standards.description: SLA standards by professional type defining response, quote, visit, and start time expectations

extensions.source_concept: Professional Profile entity — transforms User into full service provider
extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no professional profile concept. Users are either CRM contacts or agents with limited role-based fields. No structured professional identity with capabilities, credentials, or availability.
extensions.proposed_structure: Create ProfessionalProfile entity with: user_id (UUID), professional_type (Enum: 27 categories), secondary_types (Enum[]), professional_name (String), professional_email (String?), professional_phone (String?), business_address (String?), website (String?), social_links (JSON?), bio (Text?), years_experience (Int), employee_count (Int?), professional_status (Enum: active|inactive|suspended|archived), is_verified (Boolean), verified_at (DateTime?), verification_level (Enum: basic|document|field|premium), profile_completeness (Float 0.0-1.0), search_visible (Boolean), accepting_new_clients (Boolean), featured_until (DateTime?), created_at (DateTime), updated_at (DateTime). Profile transforms a User from simple CRM contact to full service provider with capabilities, geographic coverage, availability, ratings, and credentials.
required_permissions: PRO: read professional profile (all authenticated users)
extensions.human_decision_question: Professional profile field set: as specified in PROFESSIONAL_EXTENSION_MODEL.md §1.1 (core + business + status fields) or reduced set for MVP?

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no professional category taxonomy. Professional types are limited to agent_role with no support for trades (mason, carpenter, electrician, etc.) or professional services (notary, architect, lawyer).
extensions.proposed_structure: Create ProfessionalCategory reference data with 27 categories across 10 domains (real_estate: agent_immobilier, expert_immobilier, evaluateur, syndic, courtier, promoteur; construction: electricien, plombier, macon, menuisier, peintre, carreleur, couvreur, pisciniste; legal: notaire, avocat; technical: geometre, architecte, ingenieur_civil; financial: comptable, assurance, banquier; security: gardiennage; administrative: prestataire_administratif; media: videaste_drone; design: decorateur; maintenance: jardinier). Each category specifies: code, labels_fr/en, domain, description, requires_license (Boolean), license_types[], primary_pricing_model, typical price range, verification_required level. Hierarchy: Domain → Category → Specialty → Skill. Assign primary (exactly 1) + secondary (0-N) categories per professional.
extensions.human_decision_question: 27 categories as specified in PROFESSIONAL_EXTENSION_MODEL.md §2.1 — confirm full list or prioritize initial subset for MVP (e.g., top 15 most in-demand categories)?

extensions.source_concept: Service capability model — typed, priced, schedulable service units
extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no service capability concept. Professionals cannot define what specific services they offer, at what price, with what terms. Service taxonomy exists but is not tied to individual professionals.
extensions.proposed_structure: Create ServiceCapability entity: id (UUID), professional_id (UUID), capability_code (String), name (JSON multilingual), description (JSON multilingual), category (Enum), service_type (String from SERVICE_TAXONOMY), is_active (Boolean), typical_duration_minutes (Int?), requires_verification (Boolean), pricing_model (Enum: fixed|hourly|per_unit|estimate|negotiable), default_price (Decimal?), price_currency (String default XAF), price_unit (String?), deposit_required (Boolean), deposit_percentage (Float?), is_online_bookable (Boolean), lead_time_hours (Int?), created_at (DateTime). Plus matching attributes: min_project_budget, max_project_budget, preferred_project_size, languages_spoken[], service_radius_km, equipment_available[], team_available, team_size_min/max, emergency_service, after_hours_service, mobile_service. Each capability represents a distinct service a professional can deliver, with its own pricing and terms.
required_states: ServiceCapability.pricing_model ∈ {fixed, hourly, per_unit, daily, percentage, estimate, subscription, tiered, negotiable, retainer}
extensions.human_decision_question: Pricing model selection: 10 models as specified (fixed, hourly, per_unit, daily, percentage, estimate, subscription, tiered, negotiable, retainer) or simplified subset for MVP?

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no license tracking. Professional credentials are not captured, verified, or linked to service delivery capabilities.
extensions.proposed_structure: Create License entity: id (UUID), professional_id (UUID), license_type (Enum: professional_card|trade_license|RCCM|business_permit|municipal_license|sector_specific), license_number (String), license_name (String), issuing_authority (String), category (String?), issued_at (DateTime), expires_at (DateTime?), is_renewable (Boolean), renewal_reminder_days (Int?), status (Enum: active|expired|suspended|revoked|pending_renewal), verification_status (Enum: unverified|pending|verified|rejected), verified_by (UUID?), verified_at (DateTime?), document_url (String?), document_type (String?), rejection_reason (Text?), metadata (JSON?), created_at (DateTime). License requirements enforced by professional category: certain categories (notary, architect, avocat) require specific license types to practice. Auto-reminder for renewals based on renewal_reminder_days.

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no certification tracking. Professional skill endorsements cannot be captured or displayed.
extensions.proposed_structure: Create Certification entity: id (UUID), professional_id (UUID), certification_name (String), certification_code (String?), issuing_body (String), category (String?), level (Enum: beginner|intermediate|advanced|expert|master), skills_covered (String[]), obtained_at (DateTime), expires_at (DateTime?), credential_id (String?), verifiable_url (String?), status (Enum: active|expired|revoked), verification_status (Enum: unverified|pending|verified|rejected), verified_by (UUID?), document_url (String?), created_at (DateTime). Certifications are optional skill endorsements that boost matching priority and appear on professional profile. Unlike licenses, certifications do not block service delivery if absent.

extensions.source_concept: Geographic coverage model — where professionals operate
extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no professional geographic coverage concept. Agent zones exist for CRM routing but professionals of other types cannot declare their service areas.
extensions.proposed_structure: Create GeographicCoverage entity: id (UUID), professional_id (UUID), coverage_type (Enum: primary|secondary|dispatch), geographic_unit_id (String referencing GeographicUnit.canonical_id), level (Enum: city|neighborhood|zone|axis|region|department|municipality), is_home_base (Boolean), travel_distance_km (Int?), additional_fee (Decimal?), is_active (Boolean), created_at (DateTime). Coverage patterns vary by professional type (see PROFESSIONAL_EXTENSION_MODEL.md §5.2). Coverage matching: intersect demandeur location with professional coverage zones, apply travel_distance_km filter, priority to is_home_base professionals within same geographic unit.
extensions.human_decision_question: Coverage radius defaults per professional type (PROFESSIONAL_EXTENSION_MODEL.md §5.2) — use specified defaults or adjust per market reality?

extensions.source_concept: Availability and scheduling — professional schedule management
extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no availability management. Professional schedules cannot be declared, searched, or used for booking.
extensions.proposed_structure: Create Availability entity: id (UUID), professional_id (UUID), day_of_week (Enum: monday|tuesday|wednesday|thursday|friday|saturday|sunday), start_time (Time), end_time (Time), timezone (String default Africa/Douala), is_recurring (Boolean), specific_date (Date?), slot_duration_minutes (Int default 60), max_bookings_per_slot (Int default 1), is_available (Boolean default true), notes (String?), created_at (DateTime). Plus scheduling constraints on ProfessionalProfile: max_concurrent_projects (Int?), max_concurrent_visits (Int?), min_notice_hours (Int), blackout_dates (JSON), preferred_project_duration (String), weekend_available (Boolean), holiday_policy (Enum: standard|limited|full). Booking slot generation based on availability windows + slot_duration_minutes.
required_permissions: PRO: read availability (all authenticated users, for booking)
required_states: Availability.day_of_week ∈ {monday, tuesday, wednesday, thursday, friday, saturday, sunday}
extensions.human_decision_question: Slot duration configuration: 60-minute default as specified, or configurable per professional type (e.g., emergency plumber 30min slots, architect 2h slots)?

extensions.source_concept: Professional rating system — multi-category post-service scoring
extensions.current_limitation: V2 has agent rating (CRM) but no professional rating. Post-service feedback cannot be collected for non-agent professionals (trades, legal, technical).
extensions.proposed_domain: Domain 10: CRM Pipeline (extended to professional domain)
extensions.proposed_structure: Create ProfessionalRating entity: id (UUID), professional_id (UUID), service_order_id (UUID?), client_user_id (UUID), rating (Int 1-5), category_ratings (JSON: {quality, punctuality, communication, professionalism, value} each 1-5), review_text (Text?), is_public (Boolean default true), response_text (Text?), responded_at (DateTime?), verified_purchase (Boolean), helpful_count (Int default 0), reported (Boolean default false), reported_reason (String?), status (Enum: published|pending|hidden|removed), created_at (DateTime). Rating calculation: weighted average of category_ratings (quality=0.25, punctuality=0.20, communication=0.15, professionalism=0.25, value=0.15). Recent reviews (last 90d) weighted 2x. Verified purchase reviews weighted 1.5x. Badges: Top Professional (4.5+ min 20 reviews), Recommended (4.0+ min 10), Reliable (3.5+ min 5), New (< 5 reviews). Public display on profile header + search results.
extensions.human_decision_question: Category rating weights: quality=0.25, punctuality=0.20, communication=0.15, professionalism=0.25, value=0.15 as specified, or different weighting per professional type (e.g., punctuality higher for emergency services)?

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no professional verification workflow. Agent roles exist but without structured verification levels, document upload, or admin review cycle.
extensions.proposed_structure: Create VerificationRequest: id (UUID), professional_id (UUID), requested_level (Enum: basic|document|field|premium), current_level (Enum), status (Enum: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|escalated), reviewed_by (UUID?), reviewed_at (DateTime?), rejection_reason (Text?), admin_notes (Text?), resubmission_count (Int), submitted_at (DateTime), completed_at (DateTime?). Create VerificationDocument: id (UUID), verification_request_id (UUID), document_type (Enum: identity_card|passport|driver_license|professional_card|RCCM|business_license|insurance_cert|diploma|reference_letter|criminal_record|bank_guarantee|tax_clearance|utility_bill), document_url (String), document_hash (String SHA-256), file_type (String), file_size_bytes (Int), status (Enum: pending|valid|invalid|expired|suspicious), validation_notes (Text?), validated_by (UUID?), validated_at (DateTime?), expires_at (DateTime?), created_at (DateTime). 4 verification levels (basic→document→field→premium) with increasing requirements and privileges. State machine: unverified→pending_documents→under_review→[approved|rejected|escalated]. Re-verification on document expiry with 30-day grace period.
required_states: VerificationRequest.status ∈ {draft, submitted, under_review, approved, rejected, escalated}
extensions.human_decision_question: Verification level requirements: 4 levels (basic/document/field/premium) as specified in PROFESSIONAL_EXTENSION_MODEL.md §8.1 — implement all 4 levels from launch or phased roll-out (basic + document first, field + premium later)?

extensions.source_concept: Pricing models per professional type — 10 pricing models
extensions.current_limitation: V2 has no pricing model for professional services. No mechanism for professionals to define service pricing with tiers, units, or deposit requirements.
extensions.proposed_structure: Create PricingTier entity: id (UUID), capability_id (UUID), tier_name (String: Basic|Standard|Premium), description (JSON multilingual), pricing_model (Enum: 10 models), price (Decimal), currency (String default XAF), unit (String? for per_unit), min_quantity (Float?), max_quantity (Float?), is_promotional (Boolean), promotion_ends_at (DateTime?), deposit_required (Boolean), deposit_percentage (Float?), includes_materials (Boolean), includes_travel (Boolean), is_active (Boolean). 10 pricing models: fixed, per_unit, hourly, daily, percentage, estimate, subscription, tiered, negotiable, retainer. Professional type → pricing model mapping (reference table in PROFESSIONAL_EXTENSION_MODEL.md §9.3). Each capability supports multiple tiers for tiered pricing. Deposit percentages vary by professional type (30-50% typical for construction, 0% for percentage-based).
required_states: PricingTier.pricing_model ∈ {fixed, hourly, per_unit, daily, percentage, estimate, subscription, tiered, negotiable, retainer}
extensions.human_decision_question: Default pricing by professional type (PROFESSIONAL_EXTENSION_MODEL.md §9.3) — use specified ranges as defaults or allow 100% professional-defined pricing?

extensions.source_concept: SLA per professional type — response, quote, visit, and delivery standards
extensions.current_limitation: V2 has lead SLA (CRM) but no professional service SLA. No mechanism to define expected response, quote, or service start times per professional type.
extensions.proposed_structure: Create SLAProfessionnel entity: id (UUID), professional_type (Enum), sla_tier (Enum: standard|premium|enterprise), response_time_hours (Int), quote_time_hours (Int), site_visit_time_hours (Int?), service_start_days (Int?), completion_buffer_percentage (Float default 20), emergency_response_minutes (Int?), communication_channel (Enum[]: whatsapp|telegram|phone|email|dashboard), reporting_frequency (Enum?), penalty_rate (Decimal?), escalation_contact (String?), is_active (Boolean), created_at (DateTime). Define SLA standards per professional type (reference table §10.2). 3 SLA tiers: standard (default, included), premium (higher standards, additional fee), enterprise (custom, contracted). Breach handling: response → client notification + penalty credit; quote → auto-waive fee; service start → discount; emergency no-show → compensation + review. SLAs visible on professional profile and enforced through automated monitoring.
required_permissions: PRO: view SLA breaches (admin, professional self)
extensions.human_decision_question: SLA thresholds per professional type (PROFESSIONAL_EXTENSION_MODEL.md §10.2) — adopt standard/default values or allow per-professional customization within bounds?

extensions.current_limitation: V2 has OrganizationMember model but no professional-specific hierarchy (roles, commission, reporting, team structure). Professionals are flat members without organizational context.
extensions.proposed_structure: Extend OrganizationMember with professional hierarchy: professional_id (UUID?), role (Enum: owner|partner|manager|senior|member|intern|subcontractor), join_date (DateTime), is_active (Boolean), commission_rate (Float? 0.0-1.0), revenue_share (Float? 0.0-1.0), reporting_to (UUID? self-ref), permissions (JSON[]), updated_at (DateTime). 7-level hierarchy: owner → partner → manager → senior → member → intern → subcontractor. Organization types: agency, firm, enterprise, cooperative, sole_proprietorship, freelance. Add Team entity for team-based delivery: team_id (UUID), team_name (String), team_lead (UUID), members (UUID[]), specialization (String?), max_project_size (Enum), availability_status (Enum: available|partial|fully_booked). Team-based service enables multi-professional project delivery under a single engagement.
required_states: OrganizationMember.role ∈ {owner, partner, manager, senior, member, intern, subcontractor}
extensions.human_decision_question: Organization hierarchy levels: 7 roles (owner→partner→manager→senior→member→intern→subcontractor) as specified, or simplified to 4 (owner, manager, member, subcontractor) for MVP?
